info.title: asset-service internal API (consumed contract)
info.description: Internal API surface of the external asset-service, as consumed by anvilkit-export-worker. Request/response shapes preserve PRD 0008 §8.4 / PRD 0010 §8.4 exactly. Usage boundary (confirmed): the worker is a post-render VERIFIER — asset resolution before HTML emission is owned by render-origin/render-runtime; the worker may use this API for verification support only and must not own asset permissions. asset-service is external to anvilkit-platform: never implemented here; a contract-conformant mock (FR-022) encodes these shapes with auth-rejection modes. All calls carry Authorization: Bearer <INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN> (§11.1, BD-002/ADR-002). Auth failures classify non-retryable ASSET_SERVICE_401 / ASSET_SERVICE_403 with ops alerts (PRD 0010 §13).

servers.description: Internal cluster address (mock asset-service in local compose)

post.summary: Resolve a batch of asset:// references
post.description: Verification support for harvested output. Residual asset:// references in rendered HTML or same-origin CSS are an UNRESOLVED_ASSET_REF failure (FR-008) — this endpoint does not repair them; upstream resolution is render-origin's responsibility.

401.description: Service identity rejected — worker classifies ASSET_SERVICE_401 (non-retryable, ops alert)

403.description: Caller refused — worker classifies ASSET_SERVICE_403 (non-retryable, ops alert)

internalServiceToken.description: INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN — internal service-to-service bearer token (PRD 0010 §11.1, ADR-002). Dual-token acceptance during rotation.

ref.description: The asset:// reference that was resolved

contentHash.description: SHA-256 content hash (never an S3 ETag)
